results.title: Top Infrastructure ETFs (2026) – Latest Price, Returns and Insights
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > [ICICI Pru Nifty Infrastructure ETF](https://www.tickertape.in/etfs/icici-prudential-nifty-...
Content:
ICICI Prudential Nifty Infrastructure ETF tracks the Nifty Infrastructure Index and gives exposure to companies across power, construction, energy, telecom and industrial sectors. The ETF mirrors India’s ongoing infrastructure expansion that grows with government spending, rising capex cycles and large projects in transportation, utilities and core industries. These factors shape long-term economic development and reflect the direction of the country’s infrastructure build-out.

results.title: Decoding Goldman Sachs’ 2026 Investment Themes For Indian Equities - Ventura
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Share
Content:
AI, semiconductors and China sit at the centre of Goldman Sachs’ 2026 investment themes for emerging markets, placing India directly within the global capital and technology cross-currents. For Indian investors, the Indian stock market outlook for 2026 is not a binary choice between AI and China. It is about understanding how global liquidity, innovation cycles and valuation shifts transmit into domestic sectors and earnings.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **Semiconductors as a long-term manufacturing priority**
Content:
India’s semiconductor ambitions have strengthened under the India Semiconductor Mission and the Production Linked Incentive scheme. Between FY 2021 and FY 2025, electronics manufacturing scaled meaningfully, laying the demand foundation for a broader semiconductor ecosystem.
In the near term, India is likely to advance faster in chip design, assembly, testing and packaging than in leading-edge fabrication, where capital intensity and technology barriers are substantial. By late 2025, the government had approved ten semiconductor projects with a combined outlay of roughly ₹1.60 lakh crore across multiple states. Tata Electronics is among the prominent groups aligning with this roadmap.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **Conclusion**
Content:
The outlook for Indian equities in 2026 hinges on structural domestic growth based on reforms, digital infrastructure and manufacturing incentives, while being sensitive to global technology and capital cycles. AI and semiconductors offer significant medium-term upside through services, design and ancillary industries. Yet discipline around valuation and understanding foreign flow dynamics remain key. India’s structural narrative holds, but tactical volatility will drive the journey.

About the Webinar
This webinar will cover the key themes that we believe will shape the mining & metals industry in 2026.
We hold a cautiously optimistic outlook for 2026, expecting most mineral and metal prices to edge higher, supported by declining tariff uncertainties, robust demand from sectors linked to the transition to net zero and tighter supply.
Industrial policy will remain the principal means by which countries secure access to critical minerals in 2026, with most activity centred around the EU and US. Governments will push a twin-track strategy: scaling domestic capacity while locking in overseas supply via investment and strategic partnerships. We also expect robust M&A momentum in the metals and mining sector to continue into 2026, fuelled by the accelerated race for critical minerals, with industry players prioritising opportunities that strengthen their exposure to minerals essential for the energy transition, including but not limited to copper, lithium and rare earths.
Topics covered will include:

excerpts:
Section Title: ... > [ICICI Pru Nifty Infrastructure ETF](https://www.tickertape.in/etfs/icici-prudential-nifty-...
Content:
ICICI Pru Nifty Infrastructure ETF tracks the Nifty Infrastructure Index, providing exposure to companies in power, telecom, transport, and utilities. It captures India’s ongoing capex cycle, rising project execution, and policy-backed investments. The ETF reflects infrastructure spending trends, sector order books, and long-term development priorities.
 ... 
Section Title: List of Best Thematic ETFs in India 2026 > What are Thematic ETFs?
Content:
Thematic ETFs are exchange-traded funds that track a specific theme or trend instead of a broad market index. These themes can include sectors such as technology, defence, energy, EVs, infrastructure, and other long-term structural trends shaping the economy. They bundle companies linked to a chosen theme into a single tradable ETF.
 ... 
Section Title: List of Best Thematic ETFs in India 2026 > Frequently Asked Questions on Thematic ETFs
Content:
What are thematic ETFs?Thematic ETFs are exchange-traded funds that track indices built around specific economic themes such as consumption, manufacturing, digital services, or clean energy. They reflect how theme-linked companies perform collectively based on index design and sector mix. What are the best thematic ETFs?The following are the best thematic ETFs as per 1-year returns: Motilal Oswal BSE Enhanced Value ETF, ICICI Pru Nifty Commodities ETF, Mirae Asset Nifty India Manufacturing ETF, ICICI Pru Nifty Infrastructure ETF, ICICI Pru Bharat 22 ETF. Are thematic ETFs worth it?Thematic ETF performance depends on how the underlying theme evolves over time. Factors like policy support, demographic shifts, and demand trends may influence outcomes, which vary across cycles.

Fuel cells can work on natural gas and are a faster way for Data centres to get power. Primary supplier is Bloom energy and proxy to Bloom energy in India is MTAR technologies, critical components for Bloom Energy’s solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) and electrolyzer (SOEC) programs, including power units (hot boxes), sheet metal assemblies, enclosures, and the exclusive supply of electrolyzer units.

The real estate sector is reflecting the K shaped recovery in the economy with luxury estates relatively doing better meanwhile the overall Industry growth taking a hit .
